subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **1** board map of Europe
- **240** colored train cars (48 per player in 5 colors)
- **15** train stations (3 per player)
- **110** train car cards (12 each of 8 colors + 14 locomotives)
- **46** destination ticket cards
- **1** European Express bonus card (longest route)
- **5** wooden scoring markers
- **1** rulebook

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Place the board** in the center of the table.
2. **Shuffle train car cards** — Deal 4 face up beside the board. Place remainder as face-down draw pile.
3. **Shuffle destination tickets** — Keep separate from train cards.
4. **Place European Express card** beside the board.
5. **Place scoring markers** on the start of the scoring track.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **Choose color** — Take 45 train cars and 3 train stations in your color.
- **Deal 4 train car cards** to each player.
- **Deal destination tickets** — Each player receives 3 long route tickets; must keep at least 1. Also receive 1 regular ticket; must keep it.
- **Place scoring marker** on start.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Ticket to Ride: Europe expands on the original with a European map, tunnels, ferries, and train stations. Claim railway routes, complete destination tickets, and build the longest continuous route.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
On your turn, do exactly ONE of these actions:

1. **Draw Train Car Cards** — Draw 2 cards (from face-up display or face-down pile). If you take a face-up locomotive, that's your only draw.
2. **Claim a Route** — Play matching train car cards to place your trains on a route.
3. **Draw Destination Tickets** — Draw 3, keep at least 1.
4. **Build a Train Station** — Place a station on a city (costs cards).

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Claiming Routes
- Play cards matching the route's color and length.
- **Gray routes** — Any single color of cards can be used.
- **Locomotives** are wild — can substitute for any color.
- **Double routes** — Some cities have 2 parallel routes; in 2-3 player games, only 1 may be claimed.

#### Ferries
- Routes with locomotive symbols require that many locomotive cards (not just wild).
- Remaining spaces can be any matching color.

#### Tunnels
- After playing cards, reveal 3 from the draw pile.
- For each revealed card matching the color played (or locomotive), you must play 1 additional matching card or abandon the attempt.

#### Train Stations
- **First station** costs 1 card of any color.
- **Second station** costs 2 cards of the same color.
- **Third station** costs 3 cards of the same color.
- A station lets you use ONE route owned by another player into that city for completing destination tickets.

#### Route Points
- 1 car: 1 pt, 2 cars: 2 pts, 3 cars: 4 pts, 4 cars: 7 pts, 5 cars: 10 pts, 6 cars: 15 pts, 8 cars: 21 pts.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
- **Locomotive face-up** — Taking a face-up locomotive counts as both draws.
- **3 locomotives face-up** — If 3 of the 5 face-up cards are locomotives, discard all 5 and draw 5 new ones.
- **Station limit** — Maximum 3 stations per player. Unused stations score 4 points each at game end.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Trigger
- When any player has 2 or fewer train cars remaining, each player (including that player) gets one final turn.

#### Final Scoring
- **Route points** — Already scored during the game.
- **Completed tickets** — Add the ticket's point value.
- **Failed tickets** — Subtract the ticket's point value.
- **Unused stations** — 4 points each.
- **European Express** — 10 points for the longest continuous path of routes.

#### Winner
- Highest total score wins. Ties broken by most completed destination tickets.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Claim key routes early** — Grab critical connections before opponents block you.
- **Stations as insurance** — Save stations for emergencies when blocked.
- **Balance drawing and claiming** — Don't hoard cards too long.
- **Keep tickets secret** — Don't reveal your destinations by obvious route choices.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Overlapping tickets** — Complete multiple tickets using the same routes.
- **Long routes** — 6+ car routes give disproportionately high points.
- **Tunnel risk** — Budget extra cards for tunnel routes.
- **Locomotive hoarding** — Locomotives are versatile but drawing face-up ones costs your full turn.
- **Station timing** — Build stations late in the game when you know if you need them.
